title: Parabilis tests propulsion system for maneuverable cubesats
summary: Parabilis Space Technologies is testing a propulsion system for maneuverable cubesats, with support from the U.S. Space Force. The hybrid engine technology aims to enhance the capabilities of small satellites.

title: FCC approves first Reflect Orbital satellite
summary: The FCC has approved the first Reflect Orbital satellite, which aims to reflect sunlight into nighttime regions. The project has faced criticism from astronomers and environmentalists.

title: Earth observation satellites pass telecom in European space industry sales
summary: European space industry sales rebounded in 2025 after a contraction in 2024, driven by growth in earth observation satellites surpassing telecom. The findings were reported by Eurospace in its latest Facts and Figures report presented on July 7.

title: China unveils members of state-backed commercial space consortium
summary: China's government published a national commercial space consortium membership list, revealing state-recognized commercial space companies. This provides insight into which firms are considered established players in the sector.

title: The space industry is weighing ambitious hiring against heritage
summary: The space industry is balancing the need for ambitious hiring with the importance of preserving heritage expertise. The challenge of maintaining both innovation and tradition is highlighted in the sector.

title: Startup testing nuclear battery technology in orbit
summary: A City Labs mission is testing tritium-powered nuclear battery technology in orbit to validate its use for satellites and lunar systems. This innovation could provide power in environments where solar energy is insufficient.

title: Space capitalism needs more than a bull market
summary: Commercial space industry is thriving with recent regulatory activity in Washington, including executive orders and directives. However, space capitalism requires more than just market growth to succeed.

title: ElevationSpace advances work on commercial reentry vehicle
summary: Japanese startup ElevationSpace is progressing on its commercial reentry vehicle, securing customers and preparing for its first mission. The company is closely monitoring SpaceX's entry into the reentry vehicle market.

title: China becomes second country to recover orbital booster with Long March 10B
summary: China successfully launched its Long March 10B rocket and recovered the first stage, becoming the second country to achieve orbital booster recovery. This marks a significant advancement in China's reusable rocket technology.

title: Interior Department requests information on offshore launch options
summary: The U.S. Department of the Interior is seeking information on offshore orbital launch concepts to reduce congestion at existing spaceports. This effort is part of a broader initiative to explore alternative launch options.

title: Your lost dog can now call home with the world's 1st satellite-connected dog collar
summary: A new dog collar with GPS tracking uses SpaceX's Starlink direct-to-mobile network to provide connectivity from almost anywhere. This innovation allows lost dogs to call home via satellite connection.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 218 — Which Way to the Moonbase?
summary: NASA's new moonbase plans are discussed on Episode 218 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast with planetary scientist Dr. Pascal Lee. The episode explores the direction and logistics of establishing a lunar base.

title: Sci-fi action movies were better in the '90s. 'Independence Day' is full of reasons why
summary: The article discusses the decline in quality of sci-fi action movies compared to the '90s, using 'Independence Day' as an example of a better era for the genre.

title: White House appoints Harvard astronomer Avi Loeb to lead new UFO study group
summary: The White House has appointed Harvard astronomer Avi Loeb to lead a new group focused on studying UFOs, now referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena. This initiative aims to formally investigate these unexplained aerial occurrences.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches for 35th time, hauls Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket completed its 35th launch, carrying 29 Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's reusable rocket program.

title: A ravenous black hole in our backyard could be our window into the ancient universe
summary: A nearby galaxy's black hole is behaving like ancient cosmic titans from the early universe, offering insights into the ancient universe. This discovery provides a unique opportunity to study the early universe's black holes.

title: Launching from 2 continents: Germany's Isar Aerospace leases Canadian pad for $150 million
summary: German company Isar Aerospace has leased a launch pad in Canada for $150 million to expand its operations overseas. The agreement with Maritime Launch Services marks a significant step in Isar Aerospace's global launch capabilities.

title: SpaceX ignites all 33 powerful engines on Starship booster ahead of Flight 13 launch (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully test-fired all 33 engines on the Super Heavy booster for Starship's next mission. The launch could occur as early as next week.

title: SpaceX launch creates colorful 'jellyfish' in the night sky | Space photo of the day for July 10, 2026
summary: A SpaceX launch created a striking visual effect in the night sky resembling a colorful 'jellyfish'. The phenomenon was captured in a space photo and shared as the Space photo of the day for July 10, 2026.

title: Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards
summary: NASA's Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards focus on advanced diagnostics for high-enthalpy test facilities and autonomous spacecraft using machine learning for guidance and navigation. The awards aim to support innovative research in space technology and entry simulations.

title: NASA Volunteers Help Zooniverse Reach 1 Billion Classifications
summary: NASA volunteers have helped Zooniverse, a NASA grantee, reach 1 billion classifications through its online research platform. This milestone highlights the global contribution of volunteers in analyzing scientific data.

title: NASA Photographer Captures Images from F-18 Over Washington
summary: NASA photographers captured rare aerial images of the agency's missions from an F-18 over Washington, offering a unique perspective of NASA's research and activities. These photos provide the public with behind-the-scenes views of the agency's work.

title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
summary: The image shows the waxing gibbous moon from the International Space Station, which was 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar on June 26, 2026. The waxing gibbous phase occurs before the full moon, making the Moon appear brighter.

title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
summary: Radar data from the NISAR satellite revealed significant ground displacement in La Guaira and nearby areas due to the June 2026 earthquakes in Venezuela. This highlights the satellite's capability to monitor seismic activity and its impacts.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on July 14, joining Expedition 74 crew members for scientific research.

title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
summary: NASA's IXPE has directly measured the magnetic fields of the pulsar PSR J1101−6101 within the Lighthouse Nebula, offering new insights into extreme cosmic objects.

title: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports Evaluate Umbra Synthetic Aperture Radar Data
summary: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports evaluate Umbra's Synthetic Aperture Radar data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications.

title: Curiosity Sees Martian Sulfur Up Close
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover discovered fragments of sulfur crystals on the Red Planet after accidentally crushing a rock. This marks the first time such crystals have been observed on Mars.

title: NASA Scientists Take to Air and Space to Study Arctic Sea Ice
summary: NASA engineers are testing a spacecraft sensor to measure Arctic sea ice loss, with field preparations underway in Canada. The instrument is scheduled to launch in a year.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20 with 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the Starship program.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket has achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket has faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being constructed for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, marking a continued revitalization of the region's space industry infrastructure.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station represents a significant step in commercial space habitation.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar missions and establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that severely damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations at the site promptly.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to extend the lifespan of a NASA space telescope. This marks the rocket's first mission since 2021.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about upcoming activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm technology is being developed for space applications, aiming to enhance satellite servicing and maintenance. This innovation could significantly reduce costs and increase the lifespan of spacecraft.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and develop materials to ensure spacecraft can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative approaches to safeguard mission success.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' is vague and lacks context. It may refer to a spacecraft's design or payload capabilities.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for beaming power to satellites in orbit. The funding will advance their innovative laser systems designed for in-space power transmission.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The U.S. government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying the potential for building or repurposing infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launches and reentries. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies announced plans to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective space-based defense capabilities.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift from Jeff Bezos' personal investment. This signals a move toward commercial financing as the company transitions from a pet project.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology in orbit. This achievement highlights potential future applications for sustainable bioprinting in space.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for lunar missions, with launches planned no earlier than 2030. This initiative marks ispace's entry into the lunar integrator service market.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to expand the production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. This investment highlights the growing industry interest in RDRE, a more efficient rocket propulsion method.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision, which could modernize regulations in the space industry.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to LEO, including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The liftoff occurred at 3:12am from Vandenberg Space Force Base.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA is advancing its plan to support standalone replacements for the ISS by releasing a draft RFP for Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) development. The move follows industry progress in human habitation in low Earth orbit.

title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital Demo Satellite to Test Redirecting Sunlight
summary: The FCC has approved Reflect Orbital's demonstration satellite to test redirecting sunlight back to Earth using a solar reflector. This marks a significant step in space-based solar power and light reflection technologies.

title: Space42 and Skylo Test D2D Test on Thuraya-4
summary: Space42 and Skylo successfully demonstrated direct-to-device (D2D) testing on the Thuraya-4 satellite, including SMS and SOS messaging. The partners announced the results on July 9 and plan to expand the service.

title: Ovzon’s GEO Connectivity and Terminals Complete Arctic Tests
summary: Ovzon has successfully completed Arctic tests for its GEO connectivity and terminals, demonstrating technology performance in extreme environments. The company conducted three High North expeditions to validate its systems.

title: Simera Sense to Supply Imagers for Canary Islands Satellite Constellation
summary: Simera Sense will supply multispectral imagers for the Canary Islands satellite constellation project. The project is being developed by Telespazio Ibérica.

title: MDA Space Vertically Integrates in EO Analytics With Deal to Acquire Majority of CLS
summary: MDA Space is acquiring a majority stake in French Earth observation analytics company CLS to vertically integrate in EO analytics. The deal strengthens MDA Space's position in the Earth observation data processing and insights market.

title: SpaceX Files for 100,000-Satellite Gen-3 Constellation
summary: SpaceX has filed an FCC request to launch a third-generation satellite constellation consisting of 100,000 satellites for high-speed internet services. The Gen3 constellation aims to provide multi-gigabit connectivity using W- and D-band frequencies.

title: Telesat to Provide Mil-Ka Arctic Connectivity for Canada’s ESCP-P Program
summary: Telesat has secured a deal with the Canadian government to supply Military Ka-band satellite capacity for Canada's Enhanced Satellite Communications Project – Polar (ESCP-P) program. The agreement aims to enhance Arctic connectivity for military and government use.

title: Rivada Signs Connectivity Partner in Nepal
summary: Rivada Space Networks has partnered with a telecoms company in Nepal to provide connectivity services. The deal involves Mercantile Space, a communications technology firm serving the country's population of around 30 million.

title: Impulse Space and Relativity Space Join the Field for Space Force NSSL Phase 3, Lane 1
summary: Impulse Space and Relativity Space have been added to the list of eligible companies for the U.S. Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3, Lane 1 awards. The field now includes seven companies competing for these launch contracts.

title: Beyond Gravity Signs Gamechanging Contract with Ariane Group
summary: Beyond Gravity has signed a major contract with ArianeGroup, solidifying its role in Europe's launch supply chain. This deal marks one of the largest in Beyond Gravity's history.
